frame_analyses.description: Needle driver grasping suture needle; needle oriented approximately 2/3 from tip for loading
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good grasp position, needle held at appropriate angle for initial manipulation

frame_analyses.description: Left hand manipulating suture tail while right driver positions needle on Penrose drain block
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Coordinated hand positioning for suture management

frame_analyses.description: Needle driver advancing needle through first entry mark on drain slit. Entry appears approximately perpendicular
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper needle angle for entry; mark location visible

frame_analyses.description: Needle exiting drain through second mark. Suture now passes through both entry and exit points
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Exit point appears on-target; clean passage through drain slit

frame_analyses.description: Right needle driver leading first throw (surgeon's knot double throw). Right hand wrapping suture around left driver
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clear double wrap visible; right hand dominant for first throw

frame_analyses.description: Cinching first throw; both drivers moving in coordination to secure double throw knot
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Smooth cinch motion; knot appears to set well on drain

frame_analyses.description: Left needle driver now leading second throw (single throw). Hand switch confirmed from first throw
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clear hand alternation; left driver now active

frame_analyses.description: Single throw being cinched; left hand maintains tension while right driver tightens knot
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean single wrap, appropriate tension applied

frame_analyses.description: Right needle driver now leading third throw (single throw). Hand switch from second confirmed
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Consistent hand alternation; right driver active again

frame_analyses.description: Final single throw being cinched. Knot series (surgeon's + 2 singles) now complete
Laparoscopic scissors positioned to cut suture ends. Both suture tails visible
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Scissors approach clean; cutting line clear

frame_analyses.description: First suture end cut. Scissors repositioning for second cut
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean cut; remaining tail ready for second cut

penalties.description: Suture entry point appears approximately 1mm lateral to mark 1. Exit point appears on-target.

knot_assessments.notes: Clear double wrap visible; right hand leading. Knot sets cleanly on drain with appropriate tension.
Proper hand switch from throw 1 to throw 2. Single wrap executed cleanly; left driver leads.
Proper hand switch from throw 2 to throw 3. Single wrap executed cleanly; right driver leads. Final knot series appears secure.

drain_assessment.notes: Drain slit appears fully approximated after knot placement. No visible gaps or tissue damage.

confidence_rationale: Video provides clear visualization of all phases including suture passage, knot formation, hand switches, and suture cutting. Suture entry point slightly obscured by instrument angle in frame 3 (confidence 0.65 on 1mm deviation estimate). All knots appear secure and well-executed. Completion time clearly observed. Drain integrity maintained throughout.
cannot_determine: Exact millimeter deviation at mark 1 — camera angle slightly oblique to entry point, but deviation appears minimal (≤1mm)
technique_summary: Excellent expert-level performance. Completion time of 74 seconds is well under proficiency target of 112 seconds and exceeds expert benchmark across all phases. Clean knot sequence with proper hand alternation. Suture placement precise with minimal deviation. Drain integrity maintained. Task completed efficiently with strong technique.
strengths: Exceptionally fast completion time (74s vs 112s proficiency target)
All phase timings within or better than expert benchmarks
Perfect hand switching across all three throws
Clean, secure knot formation with appropriate tension
Precise suture placement with minimal deviation from marks
Efficient suture cutting with no repositioning needed
improvement_suggestions: Suture entry point could be adjusted to exactly on-mark (currently ~1mm lateral); minor detail for already-expert performance
